mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of persistent inflation above 295.0, aggressive monetary tightening forces a repricing of equity risk premiums, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >58% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 295.0 during tightening phases, or the mechanism fails to separate from baseline market drift.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following positive INDPRO revisions, or the signal exhibits zero predictive accuracy (Rank IC = 0) across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust labor market expansion and sustained consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in >60% of triggered months, or payroll growth above 200k consistently coincides with inflation-driven rate hike fears that override earnings growth.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of above-trend GDP growth and elevated inflation forces central banks to prioritize price stability over growth, triggering aggressive rate hikes that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of quarters where both GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL are above their 5-year moving averages, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, simultaneous declines in unemployment and increases in industrial production confirm economic stabilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in >60% of recovery-phase observations, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during documented recovery periods.
